title: chamaeiris notha
intro: Chamaeiris notha, also known as the 'Lesser Butterfly Orchid', is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Chamaeiris notha include its potential use in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its application in wound healing and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Chamaeiris notha include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to create teas, salves, and ointments for medicinal purposes.
